01_initial_request.target_name: Hippo signaling pathway and Epithelial–mesenchymal transition (EMT) (YAP/TAZ)
01_initial_request.input_data: PubMed literature search results (85 selected publications)
01_initial_request.user_intent: Describe existing evidence of cross talk between Hippo pathway key molecules and EMT, and highlight its importance in cancer initiation, progression and metastasis

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Hippo signaling pathway and EMT are implicated in cancer progression. [Gap] A review focusing on interactions between Hippo pathway and EMT in cancer is lacking. [Decision] To achieve [comprehensive review], chose to use [PubMed] to perform [literature search] on [Hippo/EMT/Cancer terms], expecting to obtain [relevant publications].

parameters.search_terms: ("EMT" OR "Epithelial–mesenchymal transition") AND ("Hippo" OR "YAP" OR "TAZ" OR "TEAD" OR "LATS" OR "WWC") AND "Cancer"

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Search results retrieved from PubMed database.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Search results retrieved from PubMed. [Gap] Need to identify relevant studies for full-text review. [Decision] To achieve [study selection], chose to use [Manual Screening] to perform [title and abstract screening] on [search results], expecting to obtain [selected publications].
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 85 publications were selected for full-text review.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] YAP/TAZ activation and EMT induction are associated with cancer hallmarks. [Gap] Connection between YAP/TAZ and EMT needs evidence across cancer types. [Decision] To achieve [evidence description], chose to use [Literature Synthesis] to perform [correlation analysis] on [YAP/TAZ expression and EMT markers], expecting to obtain [correlation evidence].

parameters.cancer_types: colorectal, breast, lung, pancreatic, head and neck, esophageal, endometrial, ovarian, gastric, brain, HCC, thyroid, gliomas

02_agent_trajectory.observation: YAP/TAZ upregulation correlated with mesenchymal markers and downregulation of epithelial markers in various cancers.

references.relevance: Shows YAP induces EMT in colorectal cancer.
Links YAP/TAZ with EMT in breast cancer.
Correlates YAP/TAZ with EMT markers in thyroid cancer.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Mechanisms of interaction between YAP/TAZ and EMT are emerging. [Gap] Direct physical interactions need characterization. [Decision] To achieve [mechanism description], chose to use [Literature Synthesis] to perform [interaction analysis] on [YAP/TAZ and EMT transcription factors], expecting to obtain [direct interaction evidence].

parameters.focus: Direct interactions between YAP/TAZ and EMT-TFs

02_agent_trajectory.observation: YAP/TAZ form complexes with Snail/Slug/ZEB1 to cooperatively regulate mesenchymal function and gene expression.

references.relevance: Shows ZEB1 directly binds to YAP.
Shows YAP complex with ZEB1 in pancreatic cancer.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Cell-cell contact plays a role in EMT and Hippo regulation. [Gap] Mediating molecules need identification. [Decision] To achieve [mechanism description], chose to use [Literature Synthesis] to perform [junction analysis] on [intercellular junction molecules], expecting to obtain [mediation evidence].

parameters.focus: Cell-cell contact and cytoskeleton mediated interactions
parameters.molecules: E-cadherin, catenins, Crumbs, p190A, Claudin6, FAT4, Gelsolin, CAPG

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Loss of E-cadherin disrupts Hippo signaling; Claudin6 interacts with LATS1/2; Cytoskeletal remodeling regulates YAP activity.

references.relevance: Describes E-cadherin role in contact inhibition.
Links CAPG to YAP nuclear aggregation and EMT.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Cell polarity is linked to cancer stemness and EMT. [Gap] Polarity-associated mechanisms in Hippo-EMT cross-talk need elucidation. [Decision] To achieve [mechanism description], chose to use [Literature Synthesis] to perform [polarity analysis] on [cell polarity molecules], expecting to obtain [polarity regulation evidence].
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Loss of apicobasal polarity translocates Scribble, disrupting TAZ inhibition; SnoN inhibits TAZ phosphorylation.

references.relevance: Links TAZ overexpression to EMT and stemness.
Describes SnoN antagonizing Hippo kinase complex.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] YAP/TAZ regulate EMT transcription factors indirectly. [Gap] Epigenetic and indirect regulatory mechanisms need cataloging. [Decision] To achieve [mechanism description], chose to use [Literature Synthesis] to perform [indirect interaction analysis] on [target genes and epigenetic modifiers], expecting to obtain [regulatory network evidence].

parameters.molecules: Twist, Slug, ZEB, AKT, Wnt/beta-catenin, HIF1A, miRNAs (miR-375, miR-1254), lncRNAs, EZH2

02_agent_trajectory.observation: YAP/TAZ transcriptionally activate EMT-TFs; miRNAs and lncRNAs modulate Hippo-EMT axis; EZH2 silences WWC1.

references.relevance: Links EZH2 to silencing of WWC1.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Hippo pathway components are promising therapeutic targets. [Gap] Therapeutic strategies targeting Hippo-EMT cross-talk need evaluation. [Decision] To achieve [therapeutic perspective], chose to use [Literature Synthesis] to perform [inhibitor analysis] on [Hippo/EMT inhibitors], expecting to obtain [therapeutic evidence].

parameters.agents: pevonedistat, CA3, Super-TDU, AR-42, Luteolin, Catechol

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Inhibition of YAP/TAZ or core kinases suppresses EMT and tumor growth in various models.

references.relevance: Shows YAP inhibitor CA3 downregulates EMT.
Shows TAZ inhibitor AR-42 inhibits EMT.
Shows Luteolin suppresses EMT by inhibiting YAP/TAZ.

publications_reviewed.interpretation: Number of selected publications for full-text review.

cancer_types_covered.interpretation: Includes breast, lung, CRC, pancreatic, gastric, etc.

mechanisms_described.interpretation: Direct interactions, Cell-cell contact, Polarity, Indirect/Epigenetic, Therapeutic.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The review successfully describes existing evidence of cross talk between Hippo pathway key molecules and EMT, and highlights its importance in cancer initiation, progression and metastasis.
